finding: Web platform export/import undefined. The plan only addresses desktop via Electron IPC (window.api.exportSessionData/importSessionFile). The Platform type in platform.tsx (lines 23-117) has optional methods saveFilePickerDialog/openFilePickerDialog for desktop. For web, there's no file system write mechanism. The plan should specify either: (a) web export downloads a blob (memory-only, no file dialog), or (b) web import reads via file input element. Currently, the dialogs call window.api.* which crashes on web (window.api is undefined outside Electron renderer).
repair_path: Add exportSessionData/importSessionFile as optional methods on Platform type (platform.tsx:23). Implement for desktop via window.api, for web via blob download + file input element. The dialog components should use usePlatform() instead of window.api directly.

finding: Sanitization is misleading — v1 strips session-level metadata (permission, share.url, path) but defers message-content sanitization to v2. However, the most sensitive data (API keys, tokens, credentials, internal file paths) almost always appears in user messages and tool outputs, not session metadata. A user who toggles 'Remove permissions and local paths' will reasonably believe sensitive content is removed, but the actual private data in messages is untouched. This is a trust/safety issue, not a 'defer to v2' feature gap.
repair_path: Either: (a) clearly label the sanitize toggle as 'Remove session metadata only (messages not sanitized)', or (b) implement basic message-content sanitization in v1 (regex patterns for common API keys, bearer tokens, file paths) and flag the sanitized message as 'best-effort' in the schema.

finding: importSession service access pattern is unspecified. The plan shows 'Session.use.importSession(data)' in fix-B, but Session.use is serviceUse(Service) which exposes only the Interface methods (session.ts:451-500). If importSession is a standalone function in session-export.ts, it cannot be called via Session.use. It needs to either: (a) be added to the Session.Interface (increasing surface area), or (b) be called as a direct function import that requires the Session service Layer (which the HTTP handler already has via yield* Session.Service). The plan needs to specify which approach.
repair_path: Clarify: importSession is a standalone Effect, not added to Interface. The HTTP handler accesses it via: const session = yield* Session.Service; yield* importSession(session)(data). This avoids Interface surface area increase.

finding: Import validation error UX is unacceptably poor. The server endpoint uses Schema.decode(ExportedSession)(body) which produces cryptic Effect Schema errors like 'Expected { version: Schema.Literal<["1"]> }, actual Missing field "exportedAt"'. These are not user-friendly. The plan mentions 'toast.session.import.invalid.title' but doesn't specify showing error details. Compare with the existing v2 handler pattern (handlers/v2/session.ts:92-247) which maps errors through typed catchTag chains with specific user-facing messages. The import endpoint needs the same treatment.
repair_path: Add InvalidImportFormatError to errors.ts (following InvalidCursorError pattern, httpApiStatus: 400). Wrap import body validation: Schema.decodeUnknownOption(ExportedSession)(body).pipe(Effect.catchAll(() => Effect.fail(new InvalidImportFormatError({message: "Invalid session file format: [specific missing field]" }))))

finding: fix-B references non-existent file. The plan says 'MODIFY: packages/opencode/src/server/routes/session.ts' but this file does not exist. The actual server route files are: groups/session.ts (v1 group), handlers/session.ts (v1 handlers), groups/v2/session.ts (v2 group), handlers/v2/session.ts (v2 handlers). The POST /session/import endpoint would go in the v1 group (groups/session.ts + handlers/session.ts) since it's not a v2-specific endpoint. The plan must correct the file references.
repair_path: Update fix-B to reference packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/session.ts (add endpoint + ExportedSession payload schema to HttpApiGroup) and handlers/session.ts (add import handler Effect).

finding: IPC handlers lack null guards. Import dialog flow: IPC returns null on cancel → JSON.parse(null) throws SyntaxError. Export dialog flow: IPC returns null on cancel → success toast shown with null path. The plan lists this as a risk but doesn't specify null checks in dialog handlers. IPC handlers also lack try/catch around writeFileSync/readFileSync which can throw ENOENT, ENOSPC, EACCES, crashing the main process.
repair_path: Add null guards in dialog-export.tsx: if (result === null) return before success toast. Add null guards in dialog-import.tsx: if (content === null) return before JSON.parse(content). Add try/catch in ipc.ts IPC handlers with descriptive error rethrow so renderer gets meaningful messages via rejected promise.

finding: importSession has unresolved InstanceState dependency. The plan says importSession calls Session.use.create() and mirrors the fork implementation. However, Session.Service.create() (session.ts:553-568) yields InstanceState.context which provides projectID, directory, worktree. Fork works because it runs within an active instance context. Import creates a session without an active project — there is no InstanceState available, so Session.use.create() will fail. The plan must address how importSession creates the session without a live InstanceState.
repair_path: Either: (a) make importSession accept explicit projectID, directory, worktree parameters from the imported ExportedSession data (adding a projectID field to the schema), or (b) use createNext directly with injected parameters instead of Session.use.create(), or (c) create a synthetic InstanceState scope for the duration of import. Option (a) is the cleanest — it makes importSession a standalone Effect service method.

finding: exportSession() Effect function has no call site in v1. The plan creates exportSession() in fix-A as a pure Effect composing Session.use.get() + Session.use.messages(), but the export dialog composes data in the renderer via SDK client calls, not through this Effect function. There is no server-side export endpoint. The function is dead code unless a server export endpoint or CLI command is also created.
repair_path: Either: (a) remove exportSession() from session-export.ts and only keep the ExportedSession schema + sanitize utility (both are used by the dialog), or (b) add a GET /api/session/:sessionID/export server endpoint that calls exportSession(), or (c) defer exportSession() to v2 when a server-side export endpoint is needed. Option (a) is cleanest for v1.
